title: A.I. Is Reshaping the Economy. Good Luck Measuring How.
summary: Some data suggest artificial intelligence is already causing job losses. Other sources show the opposite. Why is it so hard to figure out what’s going on?

title: Can Companies Embrace A.I. Without Layoffs? This One Says It Is Trying to.
summary: The German software giant SAP says it is betting that employees can reinvent jobs instead of eliminating them. Experts are divided on whether it will work.

title: A Quiet Surge in ICE Arrests, and Trump’s First Flight on Plane Given by Qatar
summary: Plus, the fight over “Y.M.C.A.”

title: Putin Retaliates With New Strikes After Ukraine Takes the War to Russia
summary: Ukraine is taking the war to Russia, but so far President Vladimir V. Putin’s response has been to keep attacking, including with deadly ballistic missile and drone strikes in Kyiv on Thursday.

title: Drones Over Europe Raise Concerns About Russian Activities
summary: A new study, backed up by analysts and political leaders, says frequent drone flights over NATO military assets mark a Russian campaign to probe defenses and gather information.

title: Republican Senate Candidates Start Their Own Super PACs to Gain Cash Advantage
summary: Most of the party’s top candidates are starting their own super PACs instead of relying on a powerful group run by Washington leaders. The move allows them to seize control of their financial destinies.

title: Republicans’ Home-Field Edge for Senate Seems Just Enough for Now
summary: Democratic candidates are generally popular, Times/Siena polling finds, but retaking the Senate remains a big challenge.

title: Ocasio-Cortez Endorses Abdul El-Sayed in Crucial Michigan Senate Race
summary: The endorsement is the first in a contested Senate primary by Representative Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez this year, in a state that Democrats believe they must hold this fall to win a Senate majority.

title: Immigrant Arrests Surge to 10,000 in 5 Days as ICE Clamps Down
summary: The agency has doubled its daily arrest numbers without the fanfare of last year’s large urban operations, sowing fear in immigrant communities.

title: Iran Prepares to Bury Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Killed at Iran War’s Outset
summary: Long-delayed funeral ceremonies for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, killed during U.S.-Israeli strikes at the war’s outset, are set to begin Friday. For the regime, it is a critical moment to demonstrate that it has endured.

title: U.S. Says Iran May Swap Ideology For Investment. History Shows Otherwise
summary: Senior U.S. officials have said Iran would be richly rewarded for changing its stance on the United States. Iran’s leaders have rejected such a bargain in the past.

title: U.S. Resumes Dollar Transfers to Iraq After Monthslong Suspension
summary: The Trump administration had halted the shipments to Iraq as part of its efforts to pressure the Baghdad government to distance itself from Iran.

title: Crypto Brought Trump a Huge Windfall, Even as Many Investors Lost Big
summary: President Trump and his family reaped vast financial rewards from a memecoin that generated losses for hundreds of thousands of investors.

title: Does Trump Worry About Conflicts of Interest? ‘I Found Out That Nobody Cared.’
summary: American presidents have generally tried to avoid appearing to profit from the office. President Trump has chosen a different path.

title: The Key Ways Trump’s Financial Interests Intersect With Government Policy
summary: President Trump’s business holdings, which garnered him more than $2 billion last year, create potential conflicts of interest surpassing any predecessor.

title: The Not-So-Simple Logistics Behind Taylor Swift’s N.Y.C. Bash
summary: The pop star is poised to host a wedding celebration at Madison Square Garden. The logistics in Midtown Manhattan over the July 4 weekend are complex.

title: Swifties Delight in Taylor’s Wedding Day
summary: Fans see, in the pop star, reflections of their own lives: hope, heartbreak and now, finally, happiness.

title: Trump Remakes Washington, D.C., Into a Maze of Fences and Guard Members
summary: President Trump seems to have turned swaths of the city into either a construction zone or an armed camp as he seeks to prove that he alone can improve it.

title: Boston Is Drinking Like It’s 1776. The Founding Fathers Would Be Proud.
summary: A summer of celebratory drinking is underway in the birthplace of the American Revolution. But however tipsy the tourists get, the founders probably had them beat.

title: What America’s 250th Means to Mamdani
summary: Mayor Zohran Mamdani has not actively pushed New York City’s role in the nation’s anniversary celebrations, in part because of the complexities he sees in the country’s past and present.

title: Scientists React to the Lab-Made, Yet Lifelike, SpudCell
summary: This cell-like structure can grow, feed, divide and compete. Researchers ponder what it means for the future of synthetic biology and our definition of “life.”

title: The Transgender Sports Decision Was About Something Deeper Than Law
summary: It’s never just a game.

title: An American Mosaic
summary: We explore how people in the U.S. identify their ancestry.

title: Plans for Unrealized ‘Trump Castle’ Surface 40 Years Later
summary: The 800-foot-tall residential development was designed by the architects Philip Johnson and John Burgee, but scrapped before construction. An archivist saved the rough draft.
